timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $109.9B (+22% YoY); Google Cloud +63%; management cites Cloud backlog above $460B
FY2025 capex $52.5B; management guides $180–190B capex in 2026 for AI infrastructure build-out
FY2025 revenue $402.8B (+15%); consolidated operating income $129.0B; owner free cash flow ~$72.8B
Segment FY2025: Google Services OI $139.4B; Google Cloud OI $13.9B; Other Bets operating loss $7.5B
First cash dividend declared ($0.20 per share quarterly); ongoing buyback program
Alphabet restructured reporting: Google Services, Google Cloud, Other Bets, Alphabet-level activities
Sundar Pichai consolidated CEO role over Google and Alphabet; cost discipline emphasis begins
Larry Page and Sergey Brin stepped back from day-to-day; Pichai CEO of Alphabet and Google
Alphabet holding company created; Google Inc. becomes subsidiary; Class A (GOOGL) and Class C (GOOG) share structure
Class C (GOOG) shares issued via stock split; non-voting economic rights equivalent to Class A
Google IPO; dual-class structure with super-voting Class B held by founders

industry.structure: Global digital advertising and cloud infrastructure are oligopolistic: Google, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft compete for ad budgets and enterprise AI workloads. Search and YouTube command dominant query share; Cloud is a three-way race with AWS and Azure.
industry.share_shift: Google Cloud is gaining share (+63% Q1 2026 revenue) on AI workload demand; Search revenue remains resilient (+19% Q1) but faces long-term interface risk from AI-native discovery. Hyperscaler capex arms race favors balance-sheet incumbents.
industry.trend: 2026 capex guide of $180–190B signals peak infrastructure spend; owner returns depend on Cloud backlog conversion and Search monetization through AI Overviews. EU antitrust remedies and custom silicon (TPU) are active strategic variables.
